name: M.A. in History

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: No courses and no plan-of-study groups were parsed for this 30-credit M.A. (referencedCourses 0, requirementGroups 0), so curricular progression cannot be demonstrated on the catalog page at all. For a full degree this absence of any visible structure is a substantive gap rather than a neutral note.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the required seminars and elective structure (ideally a historiography/methods core leading to a thesis or capstone) so the degree's progression is legible.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: With referencedCourses 0 and deepestPrereqChain 0 there is no prerequisite information to evaluate; the clean missingCourseCount 0 is vacuous because no course codes were parsed. A humanities M.A. reasonably has shallow prerequisites, but none are shown.
dimensions.suggestion: List the core seminars and any sequencing (e.g., a methods/historiography course taken before the thesis) so students can see the intended order.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives are present in the data, which is a real finding for a graduate catalog page.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish learning outcomes covering historical research, historiographic argument, and written/oral communication.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is 30, which is a conventional and transparent M.A. total, but with no course list or requirement groups the split among required seminars, electives, and thesis credits is invisible, and creditReconcileDelta is null.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the credit breakdown (e.g., core seminars vs. electives vs. thesis/capstone) so the 30 credits are accounted for.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The catalog data for this degree contains no program description, no course list, and no plan of study; only the name, degree, and a 30-credit total are present, leaving the page almost entirely empty.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a program description, a course list, and a term-by-term plan of study so the page conveys what the M.A. actually involves.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Currency cannot be assessed because no courses were parsed; a prospective student cannot tell whether the program engages digital, public, or history-of-technology methods that define a contemporary M.A.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the actual course list so modern emphases (digital humanities, public history, history of science and technology) are visible and can be judged.

descriptionQuality.justification: No courses were parsed for this program (referencedCourses 0, empty sampleCourses), so there are no descriptions to assess; the absence is itself a serious catalog-completeness problem for a degree.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: With zero parsed courses there are no prerequisites to evaluate; missingCourseCount 0 is technically clean but reflects that nothing was captured rather than well-formed prerequisites.

missingTopics.rationale: A required methods/historiography core is standard for an M.A. in History but no course is visible in the parsed data.

missingTopics.topic: Digital humanities and computational text analysis
missingTopics.rationale: At a technological university a digital-humanities offering is an expected differentiator, yet none appears in the catalog data.

missingTopics.topic: Public history, archives, and museum practice
missingTopics.rationale: Applied public-history training drives non-academic careers and should be an explicit track.

missingTopics.topic: History of science, technology, and medicine
missingTopics.rationale: This is NJIT's natural niche and a likely strength of the program, but it is not surfaced in any parsed course.
Data-driven and spatial approaches to history are increasingly central and fit the institution's technical profile.

emergingTrends.rationale: LLM-based OCR, transcription, and text mining are reshaping how historians work with sources and could anchor a methods course.

emergingTrends.trend: History of computing, AI, and data
emergingTrends.rationale: Growing scholarly and public interest in the history of technology aligns tightly with NJIT's identity.
A fast-growing subfield that connects the humanities to the university's environmental-science strengths.
Podcasts, digital exhibits, and online public scholarship are now core professional outputs for historians.
Bridging history with STS and data ethics broadens career pathways and interdisciplinary appeal.

topSuggestions: Publish the course list and a term-by-term plan of study; the page currently exposes no courses at all (referencedCourses 0).
Add a program description so prospective students understand the M.A.'s scope and emphases.
Publish program-level learning outcomes for the degree.
Make the 30-credit distribution explicit (core seminars, electives, thesis/capstone).
Surface a historiography/methods core and a history-of-technology or digital-humanities emphasis to differentiate the program.
